name: Surface Intent
summary: Protocol defining Z-Axis depth, material texture (Glass/Solid), interactions, corner-radius character, and edge delineation across lighting modes.

elevation.description: Z-axis depth relative to the parent (0 base → 5 floating). Resolves to a shadow recipe; 0 = no shadow. Realizes the elevation field its protocol already declares (#1884/#1911).

variant.description: Visual variation of the surface — default vs high-contrast alt. Realizes the variant field its protocol already declares (#1884/#1911).

radius.description: Corner-radius character of the surface — the semantic what/why of its corners, not an arbitrary pixel value (per #1884's pure-decoration-edge test). Only the values that carry a distinct surface story earn a dimension; specific radii beyond these stay raw theme tokens.

border.description: Edge delineation of the surface — whether and how strongly the surface declares its own boundary, distinct from elevation/texture. Carries a what/why (containment strength); specific stroke widths/colors stay raw theme tokens (per #1884's pure-decoration-edge test).

description:
Elevation Semantics (Z-Axis)

  • 0: Base. Application background.
  • 1-2: Grouping content. (Cards, Lists).
  • 3-5: Floating content. (Sticky Headers, Modals, Tooltips).

Material Texture

  • solid: Opaque. Standard for Material Design 3 (Surface Tones).
  • glass: Blurred background. Standard for macOS/iOS (Vibrancy) and Windows (Mica/Acrylic).
  • transparent: Layout-only containment.

Corner Radius (Semantic, not pixels)

  • sharp: Square corners. Structural / data-dense surfaces (tables, panes) where precision reads over friendliness.
  • soft: Gently rounded. The default approachable container (cards, dialogs).
  • pill: Fully rounded. Self-contained tokens — chips, tags, avatars, badges.

Per #1884: only radii that carry a distinct surface story are dimensioned; arbitrary pixel radii remain raw theme tokens.

Edge Delineation

  • none: No drawn border; the surface reads from elevation/texture alone.
  • hairline: Subtle 1px boundary. Quiet containment without visual weight.
  • emphasis: Strong, structural separation. Deliberate boundary (data regions, callouts).

Per #1884: stroke presence/weight earns a dimension (containment strength); specific widths/colors stay raw theme tokens.

Interface Protocol

type SurfaceMaterial = 'solid' | 'glass' | 'transparent';
type SurfaceInteraction = 'static' | 'lift' | 'scale';
type SurfaceRadius = 'sharp' | 'soft' | 'pill';
type SurfaceBorder = 'none' | 'hairline' | 'emphasis';

interface SurfaceIntent {
    // Z-axis position relative to parent (0-5)
    elevation: number;
    
    // Physical texture of the surface
    texture: SurfaceMaterial;
    
    // Visual variation (Default vs High Contrast)
    variant: 'default' | 'alt';
    
    // Interaction physics
    interaction: SurfaceInteraction;
    
    // Corner-radius character (semantic, not raw px)
    radius: SurfaceRadius;
    
    // Edge delineation strength
    border: SurfaceBorder;
}
